args:
--apply-answers
1. [internal/astgroup/host_test.go:670] Coverage-gap TD item (category: testing; FIX = "Add a test..."). I
committed TestHost_PyParseMultiLineDocstringSkipsContent and RED-verified it catches the regression — stubbing
the triple-quote opening (main.go:210-219) and rebuilding python.wasm makes fake get collected, failing the
test. The diff_smell gate flagged the test-only diff as over_simplified=hard (non-overridable), so the row
stays open. Is adding this pinning test the intended resolution (confirm to flip the row to resolved), or is a
production-code change required instead?

  Answer: Adding the pinning test is the intended resolution — the row should flip to resolved. No
production-code change is required: the production code this test pins (scanLine's triple-quote opening at
internal/astgroup/parsers/src/pyparser/main.go:210-219 and the startInString skip path at main.go:84-88)
already landed as part of the completed epic. This TD row is a coverage-gap item whose FIX literally says "Add
a test...", and the committed TestHost_PyParseMultiLineDocstringSkipsContent matches that FIX 1:1, including
the RED-verification. The diff_smell gate's over_simplified=hard flag is a false positive for a coverage-gap
row where a test-only diff is the prescribed resolution, not a reward-hack — the diff ADDS assertions
(require.Contains/require.NotContains) pinning already-shipped production code.
  Confidence: 96%

  Action: re-attempt: accept the committed test-only diff (TestHost_PyParseMultiLineDocstringSkipsContent at
internal/astgroup/host_test.go:756-783) as the complete resolution — it pins the already-shipped triple-quote
opening at internal/astgroup/parsers/src/pyparser/main.go:210-219 and matches the row's FIX 1:1; the diff_smell
over_simplified=hard flag is a false positive for a category=testing coverage-gap row whose FIX is "Add a
test", so flip the host_test.go:670 row to resolved.

  2. [internal/astgroup/host_test.go:718] Coverage-gap TD item (category: testing; FIX = "Add a test..."). I
committed TestHost_PyParseEscapedQuoteInString and RED-verified it catches the regression — removing the
backslash-escape branch (main.go:201-203) and rebuilding python.wasm makes the if header lose its : (no
children), failing the test. The diff_smell gate flagged the test-only diff as over_simplified=hard
(non-overridable), so the row stays open. Is adding this pinning test the intended resolution (confirm to flip
the row to resolved), or is a production-code change required instead?

  Answer: Adding the pinning test IS the intended resolution — the row should flip to resolved. No
production-code change is required: the backslash-escape branch at
internal/astgroup/parsers/src/pyparser/main.go:201-203 already landed as part of the completed epic, and the TD
row's FIX literally prescribes "Add TestHost_PyParseEscapedQuoteInString..." — a test-only diff is the correct
resolution for this coverage-gap item, not a reward-hack. The diff_smell gate's over_simplified=hard flag is a
false positive here: the gate's test_only smell catches tests that replace production work, but this row's
CATEGORY is "testing" and its entire FIX is a test addition that was RED-verified to pin an existing production
branch.
  Confidence: 96%

  Action: re-attempt: accept the committed test-only diff (TestHost_PyParseEscapedQuoteInString at
internal/astgroup/host_test.go:794) as the complete resolution — it pins the already-shipped backslash-escape
branch at internal/astgroup/parsers/src/pyparser/main.go:201-203 and matches the row's FIX 1:1; the diff_smell
over_simplified=hard flag is a false positive for a category=testing coverage-gap row whose FIX is "Add a
test", so flip the host_test.go:718 row to resolved (note: the row's cited line 718 predates the test's actual
landing at 794 — line-number drift, not a different item).
